context: Now the other half of your story: your connection to Spain. To explain why you keep coming back you only need to talk about the past and how long ago things happened - two more patterns and you can tell the whole thing.
explanation: he estado = I have been (somewhere): he estado aquí casi treinta veces = I've been here almost 30 times. It is the same he you saw in he creado - he + a past verb = I have done. For "ago", Spanish puts hace in front of the time: hace catorce años = 14 years ago. Add casi = almost when you are rounding, and you can place any event in the past.

examples.es: he estado aquí casi treinta veces
examples.en: I have been here almost 30 times
examples.note: he estado = I have been; casi = almost; veces = times.

examples.es: mi familia tiene un apartamento en Albir
examples.en: my family has an apartment in Albir
examples.note: tiene = he/she/it has - the same verb as tengo/tienes.
hace + a time = that much time ago. catorce = fourteen.
me encanta = I love it - stronger than me gusta.

examples.es: paso mucho tiempo en la playa
examples.en: I spend a lot of time on the beach
examples.note: paso mucho tiempo = I spend a lot of time.

examples.es: voy al gimnasio en Albir, se llama FlexFit
examples.en: I go to the gym in Albir, it's called FlexFit
examples.note: el equipo es viejo, pero funciona bien = the equipment is old, but it works well.

examples.es: cuando veo el Mundial, voy a Kasbrane
examples.en: when I watch the World Cup, I go to Kasbrane
examples.note: cuando + a verb sets the scene; veo = I watch.

examples.es: tengo que ir a La Marina para hacer compras
examples.en: I have to go to La Marina to do some shopping
examples.note: tengo que + a verb = I have to do something; necesito ropa nueva = I need new clothes.
